example: I need to renew my driver's license next month.
mnemonic: Re(다시) + new(새롭게). 낡은 것을 다시 새것으로 바꾸는 것.

example: You must apply for the visa in person.
mnemonic: 이메일이나 전화가 아니라 사람(person)이 그 안에(in) 직접 나타나는 것.

example: I was about to leave when the phone rang.
mnemonic: 어떤 지점 '주변(about)'에 딱 붙어 있어서 곧 벌어질 일.

example: Don't throw away those old magazines.
mnemonic: 물건을 던져서(throw) 멀리(away) 보내버리는 것.

example: Keep still while I take your photograph.
mnemonic: 여전히(still) 움직이지 않고 그대로 멈춰있는 상태.

example: Have you seen the latest issue of Harvard Business Review?
mnemonic: '문제'라는 뜻도 있지만, 정기적으로 밖으로 나오는 '간행물'을 뜻하기도 합니다.

example: I decided to enroll in an online AI course.
mnemonic: 명단(roll) 안에(en) 이름을 올리는 것.

example: They donate a portion of their profits to charity every year.
mnemonic: 돈(don)을 내이트(ate/냈대). 아낌없이 주는 것.

example: We should help those in need during the winter.
mnemonic: 필요(need)가 가득한 상태 안에(in) 있는 사람들.

example: Feel free to drop by my office anytime.
mnemonic: 가던 길에 물방울이 톡 떨어지듯(drop) 잠깐 방문하는 것.

example: The technology is changing more rapidly than ever.
mnemonic: Rapid(빠른) + ly. '래피드' 기차처럼 아주 빠른 속도.

example: To succeed in business, you sometimes have to take a risk.
mnemonic: 위험(risk)을 내 것으로 가져와(take) 직접 부딪히는 것.

example: The river is deceptively deep.
mnemonic: deceive (속이다) ➔ 내 눈을 속일 정도로 ➔ (실제로는 달라서) 믿을 수 없게도

example: He clumsily spilled coffee on his laptop.
mnemonic: 손재주가 없고 덤벙거리는(clumsy) 모습.

example: I end up with a lot of work after the meeting.
mnemonic: 결국 마지막 끝(end)에 가서 손에 쥐게 되는 것.

example: The marathon runners were suffering from exhaustion.
mnemonic: 에너지를 다 쓰고 배기가스(exhaust)만 남은 상태.

example: I look forward to meeting you in person.
mnemonic: 앞을(forward) 향해 고개를 쭉 빼고 바라보는(look) 모습.

example: Please sign the permission form for the field trip.
mnemonic: 허락(permission)을 받기 위한 서류 양식(form).

example: Thank you for your kind consideration.
mnemonic: 함께(con) 별(sider/star)을 보며 깊이 고민하고 생각하는 것.

example: He took a quick glance at his watch.
mnemonic: '글랜스' 하고 빠르게 스쳐 지나가는 시선.

example: The students were sitting in rows.
mnemonic: 가로줄(row)을 맞춰서 나란히 서 있는 모습.

example: Your presence at the meeting is required.
mnemonic: Present(참석한/현재의)의 명사형. 그 자리에 있음.

example: The company provides employees with free snacks.
mnemonic: A라는 대상을 B라는 물건과 함께(with) 있게 해주다.

example: Feel free to ask any questions.
mnemonic: 자유롭게(free) 느끼고(feel) 마음껏 행동하라는 권유.

example: We met at the appointed time.
mnemonic: 딱 찍어서(point) 정해놓은 것.

example: Her hands began to tremble with cold.
mnemonic: 추위나 공포로 '트렘블 트렘블' 몸을 떠는 것.

example: I cannot help but laugh at his jokes.
mnemonic: 피하고 싶지만 도울(help) 방법이 없어 결국 하게 되는 것.

example: She is a highly competent engineer.
mnemonic: 경쟁(compete)해서 이길 수 있을 만큼 실력이 좋은.

example: The news of the accident was truly tragic.
mnemonic: 비극(tragedy)적인 상황을 나타내는 형용사.

example: He was hospitalized for a severe fever.
mnemonic: 병원(hospital)으로 만들다(ize) = 입원시키다.

example: I usually take a short nap after lunch.
mnemonic: 짧은 잠(nap)을 취하다(take).

example: The medicine helped to ease the pain.
mnemonic: 이지(easy)하게 만들어주는 것 = 편하게 하다.

example: This situation calls for immediate action.
mnemonic: 큰 소리로 누군가를 부를(call) 만큼 간절히 필요한 것.

example: The charity is a worthy cause to support.
mnemonic: 가치(worth)가 가득한 상태.

example: Never underestimate the power of a simple idea.
mnemonic: 평가(estimate)를 기준 아래(under)로 낮게 잡는 것.

example: They organized a march to protest against the new law.
mnemonic: 찬성하지 않고 앞으로(pro) 나가서 증언(test)하며 반대하는 것.

example: How can we motivate students to read more?
mnemonic: 움직임(motion)을 일으키게끔 마음을 흔드는 것.

example: I consider him as one of my best friends.
mnemonic: A를 B라고 깊이 생각(consider)해 주는 것.

example: His performance was acceptable but not outstanding.
mnemonic: 받아들이다(accept) + 가능한(able).

example: The living conditions in the area were substandard.
mnemonic: 표준(standard)의 아래(sub)에 있는.

example: The results of the test were satisfactory.
mnemonic: 만족(satisfy)을 주는 상태.

example: Employees and their spouses are invited to the party.
mnemonic: 평생의 약속을 맺은 사람. (남편이나 아내)

example: The two companies are interdependent.
mnemonic: 서로(inter) + 의존하는(dependent).

example: We need a long-term strategy for the project.
mnemonic: 기간(term)이 길게(long) 잡힌.

example: The crowd became disorderly after the game.
mnemonic: 순서(order)가 없는(dis) 상태.

example: The office was completely disorganized.
mnemonic: 조직(organize)이 안 된(dis) 엉망진창인 상태.

example: She arranged the books neatly on the shelf.
mnemonic: 니트(neat) 옷처럼 깔끔하게 정리된 모습.

example: How do you manage to stay so calm?
mnemonic: 매니저처럼 상황을 잘 다루어서 결국 해내는 것.

example: You must commit to your goals to succeed.
mnemonic: 자신의 에너지를 한곳에 모두 보내는(mit) 것.

example: She devoted her life to helping the poor.
mnemonic: 맹세(vote)하고 자신의 모든 것을 바치는 것.
